vacancySearchResults.contactPerson: Teacher Attraction on 1800 646 391
vacancySearchResults.primaryObjective:
As a teacher and member of the school community, develop, deliver and evaluate learning programs and monitor student achievement to address individual learning needs and contribute to the achievement of the school’s goals. Liaise and coordinate activities for your teaching area within the school and wider community.

Several vacancies are available throughout the Northern Territory.
vacancySearchResults.specialInstructions:
The Northern Territory Public Sector values diversity and aims for a workforce which is representative of the community we serve. Therefore, under an approved Special Measures recruitment plan, ATSI applicants will be given priority consideration and preference in selection for this vacancy if they meet all essential selection criteria and are suitable at the position level.

Please ensure the following mandatory documentation is uploaded to your application in order for your application to be approved:
•	Work VISA documentation if you are not an Australian citizen.
•	Relevant teaching qualification certificate and transcripts
•	Statements of Service

vacancySearchResults.contactPerson: Chief Nursing and Midwifery Office on 1800 000 648 or NursingMidwifery.DOH@nt.gov.au
vacancySearchResults.primaryObjective:
The Assistant in Nursing/Midwifery (AIN/AIM) will work under direct and indirect supervision of a Registered Nurse/Midwife (RN/RM) within a defined scope of practice, to provide high quality, culturally sensitive nursing/midwifery care to clients to achieve best patient outcomes across the Northern Territory.

vacancySearchResults.agency: Department of the Chief Minister and Cabinet
vacancySearchResults.contactPerson: For further information please contact Ryan Webster on 0427 329 443 or Jodi Walton on 0401 280 851 or visit www.davidsonwp.com
vacancySearchResults.primaryObjective:
The Commissioner has two separate roles. 

Firstly, as holder of a statutory office under the Public Sector Employment and Management Act 1993 (the Act), the Commissioner carries out functions and exercises powers leading, guiding and regulating human resource administration and management in agencies. Secondly, the role provides strong leadership and strategic direction to the Office and carries out delegated responsibilities under both the Act and the Financial Management Act 1995.

The Commissioner acts as a steward of the broader NTPS and provides strategic leadership and direction. The Commissioner collaborates with agencies to build an agile, capable, engaged and diverse workforce that delivers government’s priorities and provides high quality service to Territorians. The Commissioner actively participates in and contributes to the NTPS CEO Coordination Committee, ensuring policy/service delivery co-design and portfolio linkages and opportunities are made and capitalised on and there is a united leadership voice.

The Commissioner contributes to an effective and cohesive NTPS; provide professional/specialist expertise, policy advice, program and service delivery, and regulatory administration at a high level; promote cooperation within and between other agencies including to deliver outcomes across agency and portfolio boundaries; promote, by personal example, the NTPS Values, the NTPS Employment Principles and compliance with the Code of Conduct.
vacancySearchResults.specialInstructions: Applications should be submitted via www.davidsonwp.com

vacancySearchResults.agency: Department of the Chief Minister and Cabinet
vacancySearchResults.contactPerson: Customer Experience and Organisational Improvement Unit on 08 8999 7494 or OrganisationalImprovementUnit.CMC@nt.gov.au
vacancySearchResults.primaryObjective:
As an Indigenous Graduate in a lead agency within the Northern Territory Government there is never a dull moment. The Indigenous Graduate Pathway Program offers the opportunity to rotate through the Department of the Chief Minister and Cabinet work units and projects that are relevant to your field of study, experience and interests. 
The Indigenous Graduate Pathway Program is designed to enhance the research, analytical and administrative skills you have learnt at university and through prior work experience. You will have many opportunities to broaden your knowledge and understanding of government decision-making, policy development, service design and delivery, and operations. You will also be supported in developing high level work skills by undertaking project work. 
As a new Indigenous Graduate, you have the opportunity to begin your career with the NTPS in a supportive workplace where the focus is on your professional development and training.
vacancySearchResults.specialInstructions: This position is only available to Aboriginal applicants.

Develops and provides senior leadership and reference point for best practice in matters of clinical importance across a specialist client population.

vacancySearchResults.agency: Department of the Chief Minister and Cabinet
vacancySearchResults.contactPerson: For further information please contact Ryan Webster on 0427 329 443 or Jodi Walton on 0401 280 851 or visit www.davidsonwp.com
vacancySearchResults.primaryObjective:
The Commissioner has two separate roles. 

Firstly, as holder of a statutory office under the Public Sector Employment and Management Act 1993 (the Act), the Commissioner carries out functions and exercises powers leading, guiding and regulating human resource administration and management in agencies. Secondly, the role provides strong leadership and strategic direction to the Office and carries out delegated responsibilities under both the Act and the Financial Management Act 1995.

The Commissioner acts as a steward of the broader NTPS and provides strategic leadership and direction. The Commissioner collaborates with agencies to build an agile, capable, engaged and diverse workforce that delivers government’s priorities and provides high quality service to Territorians. The Commissioner actively participates in and contributes to the NTPS CEO Coordination Committee, ensuring policy/service delivery co-design and portfolio linkages and opportunities are made and capitalised on and there is a united leadership voice.

The Commissioner contributes to an effective and cohesive NTPS; provide professional/specialist expertise, policy advice, program and service delivery, and regulatory administration at a high level; promote cooperation within and between other agencies including to deliver outcomes across agency and portfolio boundaries; promote, by personal example, the NTPS Values, the NTPS Employment Principles and compliance with the Code of Conduct.
vacancySearchResults.specialInstructions: Applications should be submitted via www.davidsonwp.com

vacancySearchResults.primaryObjective:
As an Indigenous Graduate in a lead agency within the Northern Territory Government there is never a dull moment. The Indigenous Graduate Pathway Program offers the opportunity to rotate through the Department of the Chief Minister and Cabinet work units and projects that are relevant to your field of study, experience and interests. 
The Indigenous Graduate Pathway Program is designed to enhance the research, analytical and administrative skills you have learnt at university and through prior work experience. You will have many opportunities to broaden your knowledge and understanding of government decision-making, policy development, service design and delivery, and operations. You will also be supported in developing high level work skills by undertaking project work. 
As a new Indigenous Graduate, you have the opportunity to begin your career with the NTPS in a supportive workplace where the focus is on your professional development and training.

vacancySearchResults.jobTitle: Nurse Practitioner- Dermatology / Nurse Practitioner Candidate- Dermatology
vacancySearchResults.contactPerson: Mercy Lasmarias on 08 8922 8330 or mercy.lasmarias@nt.gov.au
vacancySearchResults.primaryObjective:
Develops and uses expert nursing skills in the assessment, diagnosis and management of a person’s health needs within relevant clinical specialty area. Develops and provides senior leadership and reference point for best practice in matters of clinical importance across a specialist client population.
As per merit selection principles, an applicant’s suitability for this vacancy will be determined having regard to their knowledge, skills, qualifications and experience, and potential for future development. Whilst a postgraduate qualification is an essential requirement for ongoing appointment to this vacancy, all applicants will be assessed. If the most suitable applicant does not hold the qualification they may be appointed on a fixed term basis with ongoing appointment subject to them attaining the qualification during the fixed term period.
Selection will be made at the N5 or N6 level depending on the experience/qualifications of the successful applicant.
vacancySearchResults.specialInstructions: Aboriginal applicants will be granted priority consideration for this vacancy
